question: Does the output include at least 3 generated images representing the sequence: (1) right-half colorization result, (2) intermediate repair/restoration step, and (3) final fully restored version?
Are the generated images inserted logically immediately following the text describing the corresponding processing step?
Does the generated text explain the colorization strategy for the right half, explicitly mentioning the use of deep navy blue for the suit and dark grayish-brown for the hat?
Does the generated text describe the specific processing approach used for repairing scratches and correcting fading?
Is the first generated image a result of colorizing only the right half, while preserving the manually colorized left half provided in the input?
In the generated colorization result, is the man's Zhongshan suit colored in deep navy blue as requested?
In the generated colorization result, is the man's felt hat colored in dark grayish-brown as requested?
Is the boundary between the left (input) and right (generated) halves of the colorized image seamless, without a visible vertical seam or abrupt color shift?
Do the inferred colors on the right half (e.g., skin tones, bench, background) visually harmonize with the colors on the left half?
In the repair and final images, are prominent scratches (such as the diagonal line or marks on the tree trunk) effectively removed?
Does the final restored image show improved global contrast and color vibrancy (fading correction) compared to the intermediate colorization result?
Does the final restored image retain the specific colorization (navy suit, brown hat) applied in the previous step without reverting to black-and-white or shifting colors?
Are the generated images free from significant AI artifacts, such as distorted faces, unnatural textures, or color bleeding?
Do the facial features and identities of the family members remain consistent across all generated images?
